related_requirements.requirement: As any type of user with permissions, I want to be able to list the different playlogs of the system so that we can know what contents have been played on the different players of the system. Acceptance Criteria: Playlogs visible to the user depend on their permissions. All playlog details must be viewable.
related_requirements.explanation: This layer acts as the visual interface where users interact with the system to view information, including listing playlogs based on permissions.

related_requirements.requirement: As any type of user with permissions, I want to be able to list the different player connections of the system so that we can know which players are connected and which are not. Acceptance Criteria: Connections visible to the user depend on their permissions. Connections can only have two states: connected or not connected.
related_requirements.explanation: This layer displays information to the user, allowing them to view player connections as part of the system interface.

related_requirements.requirement: As any type of user with permissions, I want to be able to list the different actions performed on the different entities of the platform so that we can know what actions have been performed on the platform over time. Acceptance Criteria: Actions visible to the user depend on their permissions. All actions must be related to a platform entity.
related_requirements.explanation: This layer is responsible for displaying information to the user, enabling the viewing of platform actions.

related_requirements.requirement: The project functionalities must be accessible only to users with the corresponding permissions. Acceptance Criteria: Users without permission cannot access the data for which they do not have permission.
related_requirements.explanation: As the visual interface, this layer enforces visibility of data depending on user permissions defined in the system.

related_requirements.requirement: The architecture must follow a Client-Server model divided into 3 layers: Presentation, Business, and Data.
related_requirements.explanation: This component represents the 'Presentation' layer within the specified 3-layer Client-Server architecture.

related_requirements.requirement: Communication between components must be done via Internet using HTTP protocol and REST API model.
related_requirements.explanation: The component description states it communicates with the Business Layer using the HTTP protocol.

related_requirements.requirement: The Business Layer must use a Microservices architecture with an API Gateway design pattern.
related_requirements.explanation: This component explicitly implements the API Gateway design pattern as the single entry point for the Business Layer.

related_requirements.requirement: The project functionalities must be accessible only to users with the corresponding permissions. Acceptance Criteria: Users without permission cannot access the data for which they do not have permission.
related_requirements.explanation: The component role includes managing user management, authentication, and authorization before routing requests.

related_requirements.requirement: Confidential user information must be protected to guarantee their privacy. Acceptance Criteria: Users can only observe the confidential data of their user and no one else's.
related_requirements.explanation: Technical details state it handles SSL and authorization with the client, ensuring secure access.

related_requirements.requirement: The architecture must follow a Client-Server model divided into 3 layers: Presentation, Business, and Data.
related_requirements.explanation: This component acts as the bridge between the Presentation Layer and the Business Layer services.

related_requirements.requirement: Communication between components must be done via Internet using HTTP protocol and REST API model.
related_requirements.explanation: The component defines all REST API routes and receives requests from the Presentation Layer via HTTP.

related_requirements.requirement: The development framework must be NestJS with the TypeScript language.
related_requirements.explanation: Technical details explicitly state it is developed using the NestJS framework.

related_requirements.requirement: The database used must be a MySQL database implemented in the AWS RDS service.
related_requirements.explanation: Communication section states it interacts with the MySQL Database.

related_requirements.requirement: As any type of user, I want the system to store actions performed on the platform so that we can keep track of what actions have been performed on the platform. Acceptance Criteria: Each action has a date, client, user, and player assigned. All actions are performed on a platform entity.
related_requirements.explanation: Contains central logic for platform operations such as creating, editing, or deleting entities, and sends audit trail data to Kinesis streams.

related_requirements.requirement: Historical data must be stored in a Data Lake formed by Amazon S3 services storing JSON documents.
related_requirements.explanation: Interacts with AWS Cloud Services specifically sending audit trail data to Kinesis streams which feed the Data Lake.

related_requirements.requirement: The architecture must follow a Client-Server model divided into 3 layers: Presentation, Business, and Data.
related_requirements.explanation: This component serves as the main nucleus of the platform within the Business Layer.

related_requirements.requirement: The Business Layer must use a Microservices architecture with an API Gateway design pattern.
related_requirements.explanation: This component is a microservice within the Business Layer that receives processed requests from the API Gateway.

related_requirements.requirement: As any type of user, I want the system to store playlogs from players so that we can keep track of contents played on different players. Acceptance Criteria: Each playlog has a date, client, and player assigned.
related_requirements.explanation: Responsible for obtaining and processing data related to playlogs to enable analysis of stored data.

related_requirements.requirement: Historical data must be stored in a Data Lake formed by Amazon S3 services storing JSON documents.

related_requirements.requirement: Real-time playlogs must be stored in DynamoDB.

related_requirements.requirement: The system must be able to grow in capacity when the data volume increases. Acceptance Criteria: The system scales automatically while data increases.
